import DocLink from '@components/DocLink.astro';
import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
import { Products } from '~/models/site.models';

This node sends a request to a large language model (LLM) and writes the model's response into the outgoing message. A typical use case is predictive maintenance: the model analyzes equipment telemetry to detect early signs of failure â€” see the <DocLink product={props.product} path="user-guide/ai-predictive-maintenance" useTbDocs>AI Predictive Maintenance</DocLink> guide for a complete walkthrough.

## Configuration

- **Model** â€” required. The AI model to use for the request. See the <DocLink product={props.product} path="user-guide/ai-models" useTbDocs>AI Models</DocLink> page to learn how to configure one.
- **System prompt** â€” optional. Instructions that define the model's role and behavior (up to 500,000 characters). Supports [prompt templates](#prompt-templates).
- **User prompt** â€” required. The task you want the model to perform (up to 500,000 characters). Supports [prompt templates](#prompt-templates).
- **AI resources** â€” optional. Files attached to the request, such as documents or images for the model to analyze. To attach a file, first upload it to the Resources library as a resource of type **General**. Supported media types are text/*, application/pdf, and image/*; other types are treated as text.
- **Response format** â€” controls how the model's output is constrained:

  - **Text** â€” the output is not constrained in any way. The model responds with free-form
    text: prose, Markdown, or even valid JSON â€” whatever your prompts ask for.

  - **JSON** â€” schemaless JSON mode. The model is forced to produce syntactically valid JSON,
    but its structure â€” field names, nesting, value types â€” is not enforced; you guide it
    through the prompts.

  - **JSON Schema** â€” the model's output is constrained to adhere to the JSON Schema you
    provide. See [JSON Schema support](#json-schema-support) for what the schema may contain.

  Both JSON and JSON Schema ensure the model's output can be reliably parsed by downstream
  rule nodes â€” use JSON when the exact shape of the output is flexible, and JSON Schema when
  it must be precise. Schemaless JSON is not available for Anthropic and Amazon Bedrock
  models â€” use JSON Schema with these providers instead.
- **Timeout** â€” request timeout in seconds (1â€“600).
- **Force acknowledgement** â€” if enabled, the incoming message is acknowledged immediately, and the AI request is processed asynchronously. {(props.product === Products.PAAS || props.product === Products.PAAS_EU) ? <>On ThingsBoard Cloud, this setting is effectively always enabled for platform reliability reasons â€” incoming messages are always acknowledged immediately regardless of its value.</> : <>Force acknowledgement is also enabled platform-wide for all external rule nodes when the <code>ACTORS_RULE_EXTERNAL_NODE_FORCE_ACK</code> environment variable is set to true, regardless of this setting.</>}

<details>
  <summary>Node configuration JSON Schema â€” for programmatic use</summary>

  ```json
  {
    "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
    "title": "TbAiRequestNodeConfiguration",
    "type": "object",
    "required": ["modelId", "userPrompt", "responseFormat", "timeoutSeconds"],
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {
      "modelId": { "type": "object", "description": "Reference to the AI model entity." },
      "systemPrompt": { "type": "string" },
      "userPrompt": { "type": "string" },
      "resourceIds": { "type": "array" },
      "responseFormat": {
        "type": "object",
        "required": ["type"],
        "additionalProperties": false,
        "properties": {
          "type": { "type": "string", "enum": ["TEXT", "JSON", "JSON_SCHEMA"] },
          "schema": {
            "type": "object",
            "description": "The JSON Schema for the model output."
          }
        },
        "allOf": [
          {
            "if": { "properties": { "type": { "const": "JSON_SCHEMA" } } },
            "then": { "required": ["schema"] }
          }
        ]
      },
      "timeoutSeconds": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 600 },
      "forceAck": { "type": "boolean" }
    }
  }
  ```

</details>

### Prompt templates

Both prompts can include data from the incoming message using <DocLink product={props.product} path="reference/rule-engine/templatization">templatization</DocLink>:

| Template | Inserts                   |
|----------|---------------------------|
| `$[*]`   | all message data          |
| `$[key]` | a specific data field     |
| `${*}`   | all metadata              |
| `${key}` | a specific metadata field |

Templates mix freely with static text. With message data `{"deviceId": "sensor-5", "temperature": 97.3}`, the prompt

```
Analyze this telemetry: $[*]
```

is sent to the model as:

```
Analyze this telemetry: {"deviceId":"sensor-5","temperature":97.3}
```

### JSON Schema support

The schema must be a valid [JSON Schema](https://json-schema.org/) (draft 2020-12) and must define a top-level `title`,
which is used as the schema name in the request to the AI provider. For example:

```json
{
  "title": "AnomalyReport",
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "anomaly": { "type": "boolean" },
    "reason": { "type": "string", "description": "Short explanation of the verdict" }
  },
  "required": ["anomaly", "reason"],
  "additionalProperties": false
}
```

Only the following JSON Schema keywords are sent to the model â€” any other keyword
(`pattern`, `minimum`, `format`, `$ref`, `oneOf`, â€¦) is ignored:

| Keyword                | Applies to  | Notes                                                               |
|------------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `title`                | top level   | Required. Used as the schema name.                                  |
| `type`                 | any element | `string`, `integer`, `number`, `boolean`, `null`, `object`, `array` |
| `description`          | any element |                                                                     |
| `enum`                 | any element | String values only. May be used with or without `type`.             |
| `properties`           | objects     |                                                                     |
| `required`             | objects     |                                                                     |
| `additionalProperties` | objects     | Boolean form only.                                                  |
| `items`                | arrays      |                                                                     |

How strictly the schema is enforced â€” and whether every keyword is honored â€” ultimately
depends on the AI provider and the specific model used.

## Message processing algorithm

1. If force acknowledgement is enabled, acknowledge the incoming message immediately.
2. Render the system and user prompts using templatization.
3. Load and convert any configured resources (UTF-8 for text, Base64 for binary).
4. Send the chat request to the configured AI model.
5. Normalize the response: a valid JSON object is kept as-is; anything else is wrapped as a string under the `response` key. This applies to all response formats, including Text. For example:

   | Model response                              | Outgoing message data                      |
   |---------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
   | `{"anomaly": true}` â€” a valid JSON object   | `{"anomaly": true}` â€” unchanged            |
   | `Everything looks normal.` â€” plain text     | `{"response": "Everything looks normal."}` |
   | `[1, 2, 3]` â€” valid JSON, but not an object | `{"response": "[1, 2, 3]"}`                |

6. Build the outgoing message: the data is replaced with the AI response, while the originator, message type, and metadata are carried over from the incoming message unchanged. Route via `Success`.

## Output connections

| Connection | Condition                                                                          |
|------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `Success`  | AI responded successfully. Outgoing message data = AI response.                    |
| `Failure`  | Request timeout, model or resource not found, provider error, or unexpected error. |

## Example â€” Classify telemetry anomaly

A device reports telemetry, and the node asks the model whether the reading is anomalous,
returning a structured verdict that downstream nodes can route on.

**Incoming message data:**

```json
{ "deviceId": "sensor-5", "temperature": 97.3 }
```

**Node configuration:**

| Field           | Value                                                                                   |
|-----------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| System prompt   | You are an IoT anomaly classifier.                                                      |
| User prompt     | Device $[deviceId] reports temperature $[temperature] Â°C. Is this a critical anomaly?   |
| Response format | JSON Schema â€” the AnomalyReport schema from [JSON Schema support](#json-schema-support) |
| Timeout         | 30 seconds                                                                              |

The user prompt is rendered with the message data and sent to the model as:

```
Device sensor-5 reports temperature 97.3 Â°C. Is this a critical anomaly?
```

The model's response conforms to the schema and becomes the outgoing message data, routed
via `Success`:

```json
{ "anomaly": true, "reason": "Temperature 97.3 Â°C exceeds the typical operating range." }
```
